title: NPU virtual environment strategy: prefer --system-site-packages over external mirror pip install
symptom: After pip install -r requirements.txt from a domestic mirror (Aliyun, Tsinghua), the script crashes with CUDA kernel not found errors — torch reports version 2.x.x+cuXX with nvidia-* dependencies installed, but the host is an Ascend NPU with no CUDA GPU.
root_cause: Standard PyPI mirrors (including domestic ones like Aliyun and Tsinghua) distribute CUDA-native PyTorch builds when you run pip install torch. On Ascend NPU hosts, the system Python typically already has a pre-configured NPU-compatible PyTorch stack (e.g., torch 2.5.1+cpu + torch-npu 2.5.1). Creating an isolated venv without --system-site-packages shadows these pre-installed packages and pulls the wrong CUDA variant.
fix_steps: 1. Before creating a new virtual environment on a confirmed NPU host, check if system Python already has torch-npu installed: run `python3 -c "import torch_npu; print(torch_npu.__version__)"` to confirm.
2. Also verify system torch version: run `python3 -c "import torch; print(torch.__version__)"`.
3. If both torch and torch-npu are confirmed present on the system, create the virtual environment with --system-site-packages: `python3 -m venv --system-site-packages <project_dir>/.venv`.
4. Upgrade pip inside the venv if needed: `<venv_path>/bin/pip install --upgrade pip`.
5. Install only non-torch project dependencies (if any) via `<venv_path>/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt`. Do NOT pip install torch or torch-npu separately.
6. Verify the venv inherits the correct packages: `<venv_path>/bin/python -c "import torch; import torch_npu; print(f'torch={torch.__version__}, torch_npu={torch_npu.__version__}')"`.
7. If torch-npu is NOT pre-installed on the system, consult the Ascend/torch-npu installation guide for the correct NPU-native wheel URL rather than falling back to standard PyPI mirrors.
affected_patterns: python -m venv .venv && pip install -r requirements.txt  # without --system-site-packages on NPU host
pip install torch==2.x.x+cu124  # CUDA variant installed on NPU host
antipatterns: Do NOT create a standard venv (without --system-site-packages) on an NPU host that has pre-installed torch-npu — it will pull CUDA torch from PyPI.
Do NOT pip install torch from Aliyun/Tsinghua mirrors on NPU hosts unless you explicitly specify the NPU-compatible wheel URL.
Do NOT assume CUDA packages will fail to install on NPU — pip will happily install them; the failure occurs at runtime when CUDA kernels cannot execute.
Do NOT use conda create without checking if the conda environment already has torch-npu in its channels.

meta.refiner_reasoning: The initial venv creation pulled torch 2.11.0 with 20+ nvidia-* CUDA dependencies from Aliyun mirror, all incompatible with the Ascend 910B2C NPU. Recreating the venv with --system-site-packages immediately inherited the pre-installed torch 2.5.1+cpu and torch-npu 2.5.1 from system Python. This is a universal pattern for cloud-managed NPU instances and container images that ship with pre-configured CANN toolchains.

problem_description: Initial venv creation used `pip install -r requirements.txt` from Aliyun mirror, which fetched CUDA-native packages (torch 2.11.0 with nvidia-cublas, nvidia-nccl, etc.) incompatible with the Ascend NPU host. The system Python already had torch 2.5.1+cpu and torch-npu 2.5.1 pre-installed. Recreating the venv with --system-site-packages resolved the issue by inheriting the correct NPU-compatible packages.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Before creating a new venv on an NPU host, check if system Python already has torch and torch-npu installed via `python3 -c "import torch_npu; print(torch_npu.__version__)"`. 2. If NPU packages are pre-installed, create venv with `python3 -m venv --system-site-packages .venv` to inherit them. 3. Only install additional non-torch project dependencies via pip install. 4. Verify with `python -c "import torch; import torch_npu; print(torch.__version__)"` inside the venv.
